title: HTTP/3, QUIC, QPACK, Migration, and Production Protocol Defense · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: An engineering, security, accessibility, client, network, and operations review board. The board needs a defended multi-version protocol model, HPACK and QPACK limits, stream and connection error isolation, Go production-library boundaries, safe Alt-Svc rollout, observability, rollback, incident evidence, and residual-risk ownership. Planned artifact: Deliver HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, and HTTP/3 Production Defense with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension.…
objectives: Apply idle, open, half-closed, closed, reserved, reset, and connection shutdown transitions independently per stream.
Coordinate stream and connection flow control, frame size, concurrent streams, backpressure, fairness, reset, GOAWAY, and retry evidence.
Use Go 1.26 net/http and current HTTP/2 implementation for production while limiting custom work to models, frame labs, tests, and authorized disposable environments.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project H2 Stream State Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
project case — A project protocol team handles case 8913 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to stage HTTP/3 through bounded QPACK, fallback health, and explicit rollback evidence; preserve this operating constraint: the maintained Go protocol stack remains the production transfer target; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Apply idle, open, half-closed, closed, reserved, reset, and connection shutdown transitions independently per stream.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Project H2 Stream State Lifecycle.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for apply idle, open, half-closed, closed, reserved, reset, and connection shutdown transitions independently per stream. For Commit to the mission model · Project H2 Stream State Lifecycle, keep H2 Stream State Lifecycle connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Production Protocol Defense happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Production Protocol Defense condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Production Protocol Defense
steps.instruction:
Write the project evidence note for Production Protocol Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A project protocol team handles case 8922 in a regional status service with slow assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to stage HTTP/3 through bounded QPACK, fallback health, and explicit rollback evidence; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then append a second pipelined message and prove the first parser leaves the exact residual bytes. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend standards scope, byte parsing, framing, transitions, TLS identity, intermediaries, limits, state invariants, tests, observability, version negotiation, deployment, rollback, and residual risk.

Evidence target: Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Production Protocol Defense.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Production Protocol Defense, keep Production Protocol Defense connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend standards scope, byte parsing, framing, transitions, TLS identity, intermediaries, limits, state invariants, tests, observability, version negotiation, deployment, rollback, and residual risk.
